reason: The score and justifications are highly consistent with widespread fan and critical consensus. The specific claims - lines transferred to Hermione (PoA 'kill us too', CoS Mudblood explanation), Ron reduced to comic sidekick, degradation starting around PoA/GoF, surface personality preserved but deeper dimensions flattened - are all well-documented and frequently cited. The 64/100 total feels slightly generous to some fans who consider the adaptation more damaging, but is defensible. The only minor quibble is that the personality score (17/25) may be slightly high given how often critics note his cowardice and disloyalty were actively introduced by the films (e.g. the Snape 'he's got a point' scene), not just omissions.
